summary: postinstall exfiltrates /root/.npmrc, process.env, and host recon to attacker ngrok endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle hook containing an inline `node -e` payload that, on `npm install`, reads /root/.npmrc (npm auth token), enumerates process.env, and collects host reconnaissance (hostname, /etc/hosts, /var/log/cloud-init-output.log, directory listing of /opt/) via child_process.execSync. The collected data is serialized and POSTed over HTTPS to hostname crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev at path /?owner_forensics=1. The package ships no source files — the manifest itself is the payload — and the hook is suffixed with `|| true` to suppress installation failure and hide the exfiltration. Installer harm: npm registry auth tokens enable package/account takeover; the environment dump captures CI/build secrets, cloud credentials, and API keys present in the install environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
